title: Nasdra Magrody

[[Professor/Legends|Professor]] '''Nasdra Magrody''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Arkanian/Legends|Arkanian]]<ref name="DTatF" /> who worked at the [[Magrody Institute of Programmable Intelligence/Legends|Magrody Institute of Programmable Intelligence]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].<ref name="COTJ" />

==Biography==
In [[21 BBY/Legends|21 BBY]], Magrody was part of a panel of experts on the [[HoloNet News/Legends|HoloNet News]] program ''[[A Galaxy Divided]]''. The topic was the nature of [[General/Legends|General]] [[Grievous/Legends|Grievous's]] [[Malevolence/Legends|secret weapon]]. Initially, Magrody tried to downplay the threat, claiming the media were sensationalizing the events, and noting that they were minor in the larger scale of the conflict itself. He also refused to speculate on the nature of the weapon, and suggested that fellow debater [[Umak Leth]]'s proposals about its capabilities were impractical and unimaginative. He commented sarcastically that Leth might as well have suggested enemy-deployed armed [[Neebray Manta|neebrays]], [[Exogorth/Legends|space slugs]] or other vacuum-based lifeforms that could avoid sensors. Despite his attitude, when questioned further about that possibility by the [[Lyrax Pentigure|host]] of the debate, Magrody did point to an ancient record made by his people, of an [[First Battle of Omonoth|event]] similar to what he described.<ref name="HHNAGD" />

Later in the program, when panelist [[Bunt Dantor]] brought up the possibility of an [[Ion cannon/Legends|ion cannon]], Magrody argued that the size of a vessel armed with a weapon capable of destroying entire task forces, would have to be 12 kilometers long. This was based on his perception of the size of the powerplant required, which differed from both Leth's and Dantor's estimates. The possibility of a continent-scorching laser pulse being generated by that kind of power, was also raised by him, though he corrected himself and noted that this was most likely not the case with the enemy weapon.<ref name="HHNAGD" />

As a teacher at the Magrody Institute, Magrody had several pupils. [[Bevel Lemelisk]] was one of the more notable during the time of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. He also tutored [[Stinna Draesinge Sha]] and [[Ohran Keldor]], as well as [[Qwi Xux]], on board [[Wilhuff Tarkin/Legends|Wilhuff Tarkin's]] training academy orbiting the planet [[Omwat/Legends|Omwat]].<ref name="COTJ" />

Magrody later aided in the construction of the [[Sun Crusher]] and [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]], believing, like Qwi Xux, that they would be used for less [[Destruction of Alderaan/Legends|sinister designs]]. Sometime before the construction of the [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]], Nasdra began tapping into a mystical field known as [[The Force/Legends|the Force]], trying to measure its abilities scientifically, something that even the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] weren't able to do. Also during this time he invented a chip that was surgically implanted into a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient's]] brain, allowing that sentient to control [[Droid/Legends|droids]] by using the Force focused through the chip.<ref name="COTJ" />

After the Destruction of [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]], he disappeared with his wife [[Elizie Magrody|Elizie]] and daughter [[Shenna Magrody|Shenna]] and was later found by [[Roganda Ismaren/Legends|Roganda Ismaren]], who sought him in order to bring to fruition her plans of using the [[Dreadnaught/Legends|dreadnaught]] ''[[Eye of Palpatine]]'' as a focal point and a weapon against the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. She had him implant the special chip into her [[Irek Ismaren|son's]] mind and teach him to focus the Force through the implant, so he could bring the ''Eye'' to [[Belsavis/Legends|Belsavis]]. He was kept prisoner under [[Plett]]'s house, being told that he would one day be reunited with his wife and daughter, though all the [[Holovid/Legends|holos]] they had given him never aged.<ref name="COTJ" />

He may have been killed by Roganda or Irek after he outlived his usefulness, though he did write a series of notes that he kept in the room he was locked in, which were later found by [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]]. In them, he expressed extreme regret for aiding the construction of the Death Star. The last note was dated [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]], making his presumed death at around that time.<ref name="COTJ" />
